raw.objective: “to determine pharmacokinetic parameters, protein binding, and the urinary metabolite excretion of p-hydroxyphenyl phenylhydantoin (p-HPPH) for phenytoin during initial therapy in critically ill trauma patients for 7 to 14 days”
raw.observations: Parameter values for the time-variant model are missing, although the authors mention that the time-variant elimination helped explain some features in the data.
raw.structure: 1 compartment model with michaelis menten elimination
raw.equations: Vd=theta1*WT*exp(eta1) Km=theta2*exp(eta2) Vm= theta3*exp(eta3) dA(1)/dt=–((Vm*A(1))/(Vd*(Km+A(1)))) Inter-individual variability here is calculated from reported mean and SD of estimated parameter values. Although the time-variant model was found to perform better, only the time-invariant parameter values were provided, which is what is shown here
raw.parameter_values: theta1=0.70 theta2=3.4 theta3=596 omega1=0.126 omega2=0.300 omega3=0.106

structural_model.description: 1 compartment model with michaelis menten elimination

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending
Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending
Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.objective: “to determine pharmacokinetic parameters, protein binding, and the urinary metabolite excretion of p-hydroxyphenyl phenylhydantoin (p-HPPH) for phenytoin during initial therapy in critically ill trauma patients for 7 to 14 days”

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
